title: DXFFlatPatternExportOptions Object
description: Defines that a DXF export of a flat pattern is to be done and specifies the various options.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: convertToPolylineTolerance
properties.description: Specifies the tolerance when converting a spline to polylines. This value is only used when the isSplineConvertedToPolyline property is true and otherwise it is ignored. The units for this value are centimeters. Defaults to 0.01 cm.

properties.name: filename
properties.description: Gets and sets the filename that the exported file will be written to. This can be empty in the case of STL export and sending the result to the mesh editor.

properties.name: geometry
properties.description: Specifies the geometry to export. This can be an Occurrence, or the root Component. For STL, OBJ, and 3MF export, it can be a BRepBody. For DXF export, it can be a sketch of flat pattern.

properties.name: isCenterLinesExported
properties.description: Specifies if the center lines (bend line) of the flat pattern are exported in the DXF. Defaults to true.

properties.name: isExtentLinesExported
properties.description: Specifies if the bend extent lines of the flat pattern are exported in the DXF. Defaults to true.

properties.name: isSplineConvertedToPolyline
properties.description: Specifies if splines are converted to polylines. If true, the convertToPolylineTolerance value is used to specify the accuracy of the conversion. Defaults to false.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: units
properties.description: Gets and sets the units that will be used for the DXF file. This defaults to be the same as the default units of the design.

classType.title: DXFFlatPatternExportOptions.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

convertToPolylineTolerance.title: DXFFlatPatternExportOptions.convertToPolylineTolerance Property
convertToPolylineTolerance.description: Specifies the tolerance when converting a spline to polylines. This value is only used when the isSplineConvertedToPolyline property is true and otherwise it is ignored. The units for this value are centimeters. Defaults to 0.01 cm.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = dXFFlatPatternExportOptions_var.convertToPolylineTolerance

# Set the value of the property.
dXFFlatPatternExportOptions_var.convertToPolylineTolerance = propertyValue
